lessons: Deferred: add a deterministic scripts/categorize.py enforcing the higher-of vehicle formula and the phone-floor so the LLM can't re-introduce flat-% errors.
Re-confirm vehicle depreciation ceiling against current תקנות פחת next cycle.

Low-value-asset immediate-expense threshold (~1,200 ש"ח): no clean authoritative statutory source found, so it is hedged as a working threshold (confirm with accountant) and the broken Kol Zchut evidence row was removed. Re-source against ITA practice / תקנות פחת if a verbatim figure surfaces.

Added in verification-panel pass: עסק זעיר 30%-of-turnover election, שווי שימוש fixed-table lookup, תקנה 18 quarter-rate (bshcpa), company-still-bound-by-תקנה-14/18, and the תקנה 14 vs 18 distinction note.

STALE fixed: foreign-trip per-diem 'other expenses' 2026 rose $97/$162 -> $102/$171 (dnk-cpa 16/2/2026 circular; meals still 50%). Relabeled the frozen landline (2,700/26,600) and gift (240) figures from '(2025)' to '(2026, unchanged)'; they are CPI-linkable, re-verify annually.

MAJOR: added self-employed PERSONAL retirement/insurance deductions (keren hishtalmut ~4.5% of determining income, Sec 17(5a); loss-of-work-capacity; pension deduction Sec 47 + credit Sec 45A). Corrected the domain-checklist which wrongly waved off the filer's OWN National Insurance as out-of-scope employee payroll.

CRITICAL (re-Expert): the Sec 47A National Insurance deduction was stated as '~half of your NI' which over-claims on a bundled BTL bill. Fixed to EXACTLY 52% of the ביטוח לאומי component; the health-tax component (דמי ביטוח בריאות) is 0% deductible; apply 52% to the NI portion only (the annual BTL ishur pre-computes the base).

MAJOR (re-Expert): the self-employed retirement/insurance deductions are PERSONAL annual-return items (nispach to Form 1301), NOT P&L expense lines; added a warning not to book them to the chart of accounts (account 68 pension = employees), avoiding profit-misstatement + double-counting.

Deferred MINOR (next cycle): add interest/financing-cost and bad-debt (חובות אבודים) deduction rules; foreign-trip LODGING caps ($365/night tiered, 2026); professional literature/association dues; make the self-employed deductions an explicit 'Should cover' checklist item; re-verify the per-diem against the ITA annual update.
